category_title: Disengaged adolescent with behavioral history and word-skipping habits
minimal_context: Derek is reading a page of text independently during a general English class assignment.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please read this page of the text for your independent reading assignment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Scowls) Okay. (Reads the page as quickly as possible)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What did you understand from that page?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Speaks quickly in short bursts) Nothing. It's boring.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Go back and try to make sense of the words on the page.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm not rereading it, I just want to finish the task. My eyes just seem to skip over words as I’m going down the page.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please read this page of the text for your independent reading assignment.

STUDENT: (Scowls) Okay. (Reads the page as quickly as possible)

TUTOR: What did you understand from that page?

STUDENT: (Speaks quickly in short bursts) Nothing. It's boring.

TUTOR: Go back and try to make sense of the words on the page.

STUDENT: I'm not rereading it, I just want to finish the task. My eyes just seem to skip over words as I’m going down the page.

task.content: Reading a page of text during a classroom assignment

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Reading as quickly as possible, skipping words and lines in order to continue and finish the task presented, and never being deliberate about making sense of the words on the page
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Reading was an activity that caused him struggle and boredom, and did not give a feeling of confidence
related_quotes: My eyes just seem to skip over words as I’m going down the page.

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Instead of persevering through the text, such as rereading in order to make meaning, he would just skip words or lines
related_quotes: My eyes just seem to skip over words as I’m going down the page.
